checks.a_analysis: The output JSON strictly follows the text present in the source PDF slides. There are no fabricated paragraphs, sections, or sentences added by the AI that aren't based on the source document content.

There are no URLs, phone numbers, or email addresses in the output that do not exist in the source. While one URL from the source is missing (see C6), no fabricated contact information was introduced.
All three pages/slides of the source document are represented in the output JSON as major sections. Although content within those sections is missing (see C5), the sections themselves are present.
The source document contains no tables; the content is organized as headers and lists.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a major omission on Page 3 (the 'Tasks' section). The source slide contains two main bullets ('Ongoing' and 'Upcoming') with extensive nested sub-bullets detailing specific tasks (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data...', 'EPA parcels...', 'Elevation download...', 'Website redesign...'). The output JSON contains only the top-level bullet labels 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' and completely fails to capture any of the nested information, which constitutes over 90% of the slide's text content.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Infrastructure buildout
Replicate data between web servers
Replicate all map/image services between data centers
Replicate geodatabases between DB servers
EPA parcels project implementation
Upcoming
Elevation download from Geoportal imagery tab
Website redesign/integration
NC OneMap
Geoportal
checks.b_evidence_output: The list items 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' are present but have 'nested_list: null' and no subsequent elements containing the details.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: On Page 2, the URL 'data.nconemap.com' which follows 'Geoportal Website:' in the source is missing from the output. This is a wording change that alters meaning by removing the primary destination information for the user.

checks.a_analysis: The content from the source PDF appears only once in the JSON output. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or repeated sections across the converted document.

The heading hierarchy is logical (H1 followed by H2). The promotion of the bullet point 'Natural Heritage Program' on page 2 to an H2 is an acceptable adjustment for document accessibility and screen reader navigation.
The source document contains no tables.

On page 2, the footer text 'Geoportal Website:' (reading_order 7) is placed before the primary content list 'Natural Heritage Program' (reading_order 9-10). In the source document, this is clearly bottom-aligned footer information that should follow the main content. Additionally, the reading order on page 3 is non-sensical because nearly all of the page's informative text has been omitted, leaving only top-level category headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 shows 'Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com' at the bottom left, below the main list starting with 'Natural Heritage Program'. Page 3 contains multiple levels of technical tasks.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 2 elements index 2 ('Geoportal Website:') precedes index 3 ('Natural Heritage Program'). Section 3 elements are almost entirely missing content.

checks.a_analysis: On page 3, the document contains complex nested lists. The output JSON fails to capture almost all of this structure. The list items 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' are present, but their entire sub-structures (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data...', 'EPA parcels project...', 'Elevation download...', 'Website redesign...') are completely omitted from the output. This is a critical structural failure that loses the vast majority of the source document's informational value.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3: Ongoing list contains sub-bullets 'Infrastructure buildout' (with 3 nested sub-items) and 'EPA parcels project implementation'. Upcoming list contains 'Elevation download...' and 'Website redesign/integration' (with 2 nested sub-items).
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in Section 3 (reading_order 13) contains only 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' as items, with no nested lists or remaining text content.

checks.a_analysis: The URL 'data.nconemap.com' prominently displayed in the footer of page 2 is entirely missing from the output JSON. While the lead-in text 'Geoportal Website:' is present, the actual link/URL is not captured.
checks.b_evidence_output: [The text and link are entirely missing from Section 2]

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output fails to faithfully represent the source material due to the omission and misplacement of several key elements. First, the URL 'data.nconemap.com' associated with the 'Geoportal Website' on slide 2 is entirely missing from the visual body content. Second, on slide 3 ('Tasks'), the entire hierarchy of nested sub-bullets containing specific tasks (such as 'Infrastructure buildout', 'EPA parcels project implementation', 'Elevation download...', etc.) is absent; only the top-level categories 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' are rendered. Finally, the green checkmark icons that serve as status indicators for specific list items on slide 3 are removed from their context and rendered as a single, oversized image at the bottom of the section, which fundamentally changes the visual flow and intended meaning of the information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: 'Geoportal Website: data.nconemap.com'. Page 3: Nested list items under 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' (e.g., 'Infrastructure buildout', 'Replicate data between web servers...') and small green checkmark status icons next to specific lines.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the label 'Geoportal Website:' followed by blank space. The 'Tasks' section shows only bullets for 'Ongoing' and 'Upcoming' with no nested items below them. A single large checkmark icon is placed at the bottom of the section, detached from any specific text.
checks.b_evidence_location: Tasks and Data Releases sections (corresponding to PDF pages 2 and 3)

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables to evaluate.

The meaningful images (the status checkmarks from slide 3) are present in the output. While they are poorly positioned and scaled (noted in V1), they are not missing or broken. Decorative elements such as page borders and the circular background graphics are correctly omitted as per accessibility best practices.

There is no visual duplication of body content. The document title and section headings appearing in both the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body is intentional accessible design and does not constitute a defect.
